growth_records.notes: V. natriegens LOF mutant (ΔnifA) grows on NH4+; maximum specific growth rate at saturating NH4+ is Monod mu_max_NH4+ = 0.43 h-1 (Methods/Fig 1 model parameter). growth_records.notes: Anoxic mixed-acid fermentation, MDC80 = MDC (M9-derived, ref 63) + 80 mM NaCl, 25 mM glucose, 10 mM NH4Cl, 1 mM MgSO4, 0.1 mM CaCl2; 30C, 150 rpm. Fold comparisons vs E. coli: glucose consumed 1.2x lower, acetic acid 1.20x lower, lactic acid 1.06x higher, formic acid & ethanol no difference. Final pH 5.2 +/- 0.0 at 24 h. Carbon balance 6 +/- 2% unaccounted. Absolute product concentrations/yields and glucose consumed are figure-only. V. natriegens ATCC14048 originally reported ~1.06x faster at 37C than 30C (ref 35) but grown at 30C here.

growth_records.cite: Hädrich, Maurice et al. Low-biomass pyruvate production with engineered Vibrio natriegens is accompanied by parapyruvate formation. Microbial Cell Factories (2025). DOI:10.1186/s12934-025-02693-1. [PMC11951559]
growth_records.method: reported (Δvnp12 ΔaceE ΔligK mu = 1.13 +/- 0.04 h-1 vs parental Δvnp12 ΔaceE mu = 1.15 +/- 0.01 h-1; ligK overexpression uninduced mu = 0.97 +/- 0.02, induced mu = 0.63 +/- 0.01 h-1). td for ligK-deletion strain = ln2/1.13 = 0.613 h
growth_records.snippet: V. natriegens Δvnp12 ΔaceE ΔligK did not exhibit a growth rate reduction with a µ of 1.13 ± 0.04 h−1 compared to the parental strain, which had a µ of 1.15 ± 0.01 h−1 ... overexpression of ligK, with a µ of 0.97 ± 0.02 h−1 and 0.63 ± 0.01 h
growth_records.notes: ligK genetic-variant panel, shaking flask, 7.5 g/L glucose + 1 g/L acetate, 37C. Growth rates: ligK-deletion 1.13/h (td 0.613 h), parental 1.15/h (td 0.603 h), pEKEx2-ligK uninduced 0.97/h (td 0.715 h), induced 0.63/h (td 1.100 h). Bioreactor (100 g/L glucose + 2 g/L acetate + 0.24 g/h acetate feed): induced ligK-overexpression pyruvate only 8.8 g/L (78% drop), parapyruvate ~40 g/L (3-fold higher). Demonstrates LigK forms parapyruvate in vivo under excess pyruvate.
